title: Crisp Layered French Chicken Drumsticks Vol-au-Vent
description: A French-inspired dish featuring chicken drumsticks, layered with a glassy jelly and crispy puff pastry, perfect for a solo dinner.
instructions: Roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Cut the pastry into squares about 3 inches (7.5 cm) per side.
Place a chicken drumstick in the center of each square, brush the edges with the egg, and fold the pastry over the drumstick to form a triangle.
Press the edges to seal and use a fork to crimp them.
Place the vol-au-vents on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of space between each one.
Brush the tops with the confit lard or duck fat and cut a small slit in the top of each pastry to allow steam to escape.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and puffed.
While the vol-au-vents are baking, prepare the jelly by mixing the grape jelly, dry white wine, and fresh thyme in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the jelly is dissolved.
Bring the jelly to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until it has thickened slightly.
To assemble the dish, place a vol-au-vent on each plate, spoon some of the jelly over the top, and garnish with a few pearl onions.
Garnish with fresh thyme, if desired.
